_doc: Stratigraphy + structure facts. Layer NAMES/codes/roles, the ~2% dip and the cited thickness/elevation ranges are facts (Brochure 2018 fig.4-2/4-3, p.6/14/15). RENDER CAVEAT (paradata pd-strata-render): the cross-section draws the sandstone/marl interface (= groundwater table ≈ gallery floor, fig.5-24) at its REAL elevation, but the weathered-cover / marl / keuper sub-thicknesses are approximate display values, NOT the cited ranges. Colours live in model-config.json, joined by `code`.

strataDipPct.note: strata slightly inclined ~2% toward the SE; natural drainage toward Haedchen

thicknessRangeM.note: ~2 m on plateau; up to 10 m in Haedchen
country's main aquifer; at the horst crest the li2+li1 Liassic package digitises to ~50 m (fig.4-3)

liassicPackageM.note: digitised crest thickness of the whole Liassic package (li2 sandstone + li1 marl) above the Keuper, at the 20 m scale bar

thicknessM.note: digitised ~5 m (consistent across the section) against the 20 m scale bar

thicknessMinM.note: digitised ≥44 m; base lies below the section, so this is a minimum

structure.note: The Pëtschend is a central horst bounded N & S by ~E–W faults where strata are re-aligned vertically; some groundwater leaks along the faults to the Dauvebur (N) and Op der Rëll (S) springs.
